source: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 - Security and Privacy Controls

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate an access control policy that addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance.

controls.title: Account Management
controls.description: Manage information system accounts, including establishing, activating, modifying, reviewing, disabling, and removing accounts.

controls.title: Access Enforcement
controls.description: Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.

controls.title: Least Privilege
controls.description: Employ the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses for users which are necessary to accomplish assigned organizational tasks.

controls.title: Remote Access
controls.description: Establish and document usage restrictions, configuration/connection requirements, and implementation guidance for each type of remote access allowed.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate an audit and accountability policy.

controls.title: Event Logging
controls.description: Identify the types of events that the system is capable of logging in support of the audit function and coordinate the event logging function with other organizations.

controls.title: Content of Audit Records
controls.description: Ensure that audit records contain information that establishes what type of event occurred, when the event occurred, where the event occurred, the source of the event, the outcome of the event, and the identity of any individuals or subjects associated with the event.

controls.title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
controls.description: Review and analyze system audit records at a defined frequency for indications of inappropriate or unusual activity.

controls.title: Protection of Audit Information
controls.description: Protect audit information and audit tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate a configuration management policy.

controls.title: Baseline Configuration
controls.description: Develop, document, and maintain under configuration control, a current baseline configuration of the information system.

controls.title: Configuration Settings
controls.description: Establish and document configuration settings for information technology products employed within the information system that reflect the most restrictive mode consistent with operational requirements.

controls.title: Least Functionality
controls.description: Configure the information system to provide only essential capabilities, prohibiting or restricting the use of functions, ports, protocols, and/or services not required.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate an identification and authentication policy.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
controls.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate organizational users and associate that unique identification with processes acting on behalf of those users.

controls.title: Authenticator Management
controls.description: Manage information system authenticators by verifying, as part of the initial authenticator distribution, the identity of the individual, group, role, or device receiving the authenticator.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Non-Organizational Users)
controls.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate non-organizational users (or processes acting on behalf of non-organizational users).

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate an incident response policy.

controls.title: Incident Handling
controls.description: Implement an incident handling capability for security incidents that includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate a risk assessment policy.

controls.title: Risk Assessment
controls.description: Conduct a risk assessment, including the likelihood and magnitude of harm, from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction of the system.

controls.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
controls.description: Monitor and scan for vulnerabilities in the information system and hosted applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities potentially affecting the system are identified.

controls.title: Denial-of-Service Protection
controls.description: Protect against or limit the effects of denial-of-service attacks.

controls.title: Boundary Protection
controls.description: Monitor and control communications at the external boundary of the system and at key internal boundaries within the system.

controls.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
controls.description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of information and detect changes to information during transmission.

controls.title: Protection of Information at Rest
controls.description: Protect the confidentiality and integrity of information at rest.

controls.title: Flaw Remediation
controls.description: Identify, report, and correct information system flaws; test software updates related to flaw remediation for effectiveness and potential side effects before installation.

controls.title: Malicious Code Protection
controls.description: Implement malicious code protection mechanisms at information system entry and exit points to detect and eradicate malicious code.

controls.title: System Monitoring
controls.description: Monitor the information system to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks in accordance with monitoring objectives.

controls.title: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity
controls.description: Employ integrity verification tools to detect unauthorized changes to software, firmware, and information.
